Name: dynamic_precision_allocation_through_spectral_analysis
Title: Dynamic Precision Allocation via Spectral Analysis for Resource-Efficient Inference
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that leveraging real-time spectral analysis of hidden states can dynamically adjust numerical precision during inference, optimizing resource allocation while minimizing latency without requiring retraining.
Related Work: Existing frameworks like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) focus on static precision allocation based on activation geometry but do not fully exploit the dynamism of spectral properties during inference. Our approach distinctly emphasizes real-time adjustments to precision based on spectral characteristics, which has not been fully explored.
Abstract: In the realm of machine learning inference, optimizing resource allocation while adhering to strict latency requirements is critical. We propose a novel framework for dynamic precision allocation based on real-time spectral analysis of hidden states. Our approach leverages the covariance condition number and spectral decay rate of activations as proxies for quantization sensitivity, allowing for adaptive allocation of numerical precision during inference. By employing a Jacobian-based layer-skipping mechanism, we ensure minimal hidden-state perturbation and adherence to wall-clock budgets. The framework operates without retraining, making it particularly suitable for latency-critical applications. We will evaluate the proposed method against established benchmarks, demonstrating its capacity to achieve superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs and offering a transparent, reproducible pathway for efficient inference under varying input distributions.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline model with fixed precision allocation and a model using our dynamic precision allocation framework.
2. Evaluate both models on a set of benchmarks with varying input distributions and latency constraints.
3. Measure throughput (inferences per second) and accuracy (top-1 accuracy) for both models.
4. Analyze the covariance condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states to correlate with performance metrics.
5. Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of dynamic precision adjustments on inference quality and speed.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the computational overhead of real-time spectral analysis and the need for robust heuristics to ensure stable precision adjustments. Limitations may arise from variations in model architecture impacting the effectiveness of the proposed framework.
